And then we also have other varieties of tasks that require only naturally occurring data.

So the all the methods that I'm going to be talking about here fall in under the class of
multi-cast learning and so multi-cast learning is training models to do well on multiple tasks at once.
Just to give an example you could have this as an example and you could be doing language modeling
on it you could also be training a model to do tagging on it and other things like this. And exactly
how you do this can be different but the important thing is that you have some shared parameters
between the models that are trained on all tasks and if you're just training a big language
model then you'll probably be sharing all of the parameters. If you're training a
something like BERT or like you're you're pre training in the then fine tuning you might train the
body of the model on multiple tasks but have a separate classification for different tasks. So
there's different ways you can do that but the basic idea is that you need to have lots of shared parameters.
